info.title: Solomon Harness
info.description:
Open-source agentic shell — Web MVP (Phase 0) + sub-agent system (Phase 2.0+2.1) + GitHub PR integration (Phase 2.2) + scope-gated API (Phase 1.6) + observability (Phase 4.1).

**API versioning:** Legacy `/api/*` paths return RFC 8594 `Deprecation: true` and `Sunset: Wed, 31 Dec 2026 23:59:59 GMT` headers. New clients SHOULD use `/api/v1/*` (canonical). See docs/api-versioning.md for the migration timeline.

get.description: Backward-compat alias for ``/health/deep`` (Phase 0+).

schema.title: Response Health Alias Api Health Get

get.description: Liveness probe.

schema.title: Response Health Api V1 Health Get

get.description: Prometheus scrape endpoint. Disabled by default in Settings.

Liveness: is the process alive? Always 200 unless the Python
interpreter is broken. Used by Kubernetes liveness probe.

schema.title: Response Health Live Health Live Get

get.description:
Readiness: are the required dependencies up? Returns 503 if
``require_qdrant`` / ``require_neo4j`` are set and the dep is down.

schema.title: Response Health Ready Health Ready Get

get.description:
Deep health: run every registered probe with full timeout.
Used by ops dashboards.

schema.title: Response Health Deep Health Deep Get

get.description: List most recent sessions.

schema.title: Response List Sessions Api Sessions Get

post.description:
Create new session.

v1.0.0 security fix: requires ``sessions.write`` scope (was:
``sessions.read`` — REST semantics violation). Creating a session
is a mutation, not a read.

schema.title: Response Create Session Api Sessions Post

get.description: Get session by id.

schema.title: Response Get Session Api Sessions  Session Id  Get

get.description: List messages for a session, in order.

schema.title: Response List Messages Api Sessions  Session Id  Messages Get

post.description:
Add a message to a session.

Also touches the session (updated_at + message_count++) and mirrors to JSONL.

schema.title: Response Add Message Api Sessions  Session Id  Messages Post

get.description:
Return all catalog models with availability computed from env.

Response shape (per model):
    {
      "id": "MiniMax-M2.7",
      "provider": "minimax",
      "tier": "T3",
      "context": 200000,
      "available": true|false,
      "pricing_input": 0.30,
      "pricing_output": 0.60,
    }

schema.title: Response Get Models Api Models Get

get.description:
Return all catalog models with availability computed from env.

Response shape (per model):
    {
      "id": "MiniMax-M2.7",
      "provider": "minimax",
      "tier": "T3",
      "context": 200000,
      "available": true|false,
      "pricing_input": 0.30,
      "pricing_output": 0.60,
    }

schema.title: Response Get Models Api V1 Models Get

get.description:
Long-poll the broker for the next pending question.

Behaviour:
    - If long-poll is disabled → 403.
    - If a pending question exists right now → return it (200).
    - Otherwise poll every ``interval_s`` up to ``timeout_s``.
      On timeout → 200 with empty body via raising HTTPException(200)?
      No — FastAPI doesn't allow 200 + empty via raise cleanly. We
      instead return a 404 with ``detail="no_pending_question"`` so
      the caller can distinguish "disabled" (403), "timeout" (404),
      and "got one" (200). (Spec also allows empty body on timeout;
      we chose 404 because FastAPI serialises a clean JSON error
      body which is easier for clients to parse than an empty one.)

parameters.name: session

schema.description: Optional session id (informational — broker is process-global).
schema.title: Session

parameters.description: Optional session id (informational — broker is process-global).

post.description:
Submit an answer for a pending question.

Returns:
    ``{"accepted": True, "question_id": "..."}`` on success.

Status codes:
    - 200: answer accepted, future resolved.
    - 403: long-poll disabled.
    - 404: question_id unknown or already resolved (broker.answer
      returned False).

schema.title: Response Elicitation Answer Api V1 Elicitation Answer Post

get.description:
Server-Sent Events stream for Elicitation lifecycle events.

Returns a ``text/event-stream`` response that stays open until the
client disconnects, the heartbeat-max-age is reached, or the server
shuts down. See the module docstring for the wire format.

Status codes:
    - 200 (streaming): success — the response body is the SSE stream.
    - 403: ``hooks_elicitation_sse_enabled`` is False, OR the token
      lacks the ``elicitation.read`` scope.

parameters.name: session

schema.description: Optional session id filter. When set, only questions published with a matching ``session_id`` are streamed. The broker is process-global so the filter is informational.
schema.title: Session

parameters.description: Optional session id filter. When set, only questions published with a matching ``session_id`` are streamed. The broker is process-global so the filter is informational.

get.description:
Return the persisted Elicitation decision history.

Reads directly from the shared ``agent-jobs.db`` SQLite file — no
broker interaction. The broker may be writing concurrently; SQLite
handles the locking, and our reads are short-lived.

schema.title: Response Elicitation History Api V1 Elicitation History Get

get.description:
Return a JSON snapshot of all Prometheus counters + gauges.

Same data as ``GET /metrics`` (Prometheus text format), but as
nested JSON for easier consumption by admin tools. Histograms are
excluded (the Prometheus text format is their canonical export).

parameters.name: filter

schema.description: Optional regex filter on metric names. When set, only metrics whose name matches the pattern are returned. Overrides ``settings.hooks_observability_admin_metrics_filter`` for this request.
schema.title: Filter

parameters.description: Optional regex filter on metric names. When set, only metrics whose name matches the pattern are returned. Overrides ``settings.hooks_observability_admin_metrics_filter`` for this request.

schema.title: Response Admin Metrics Api V1 Observability Metrics Get

get.description:
Return a JSON deep health report (8 subsystem probes).

Reuses :meth:`HealthChecker.deep` from the singleton observability
handle. The response shape is the canonical ``HealthReport.to_dict()``
output (``{status, version, project_root, checks, probes, ts}``).

schema.title: Response Admin Health Deep Api V1 Observability Health Deep Get

get.description:
Return the last N :class:`HookAuditSink` entries.

The audit sink is DI'd via ``app.state.audit_sink`` by the FastAPI
lifespan handler. When the sink is not configured (no
``hooks_audit_log=True``), the endpoint returns an empty list
rather than 503 — the absence of audit logging is a valid state
and the admin tool should see ``[]`` (not an error).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of recent audit entries to return. Capped at ``settings.hooks_observability_admin_audit_max_limit`` (default 500).
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of recent audit entries to return. Capped at ``settings.hooks_observability_admin_audit_max_limit`` (default 500).

schema.title: Response Admin Audit Recent Api V1 Observability Audit Recent Get

get.description:
List recent outbound webhook DLQ entries (Phase 4.13B Drift 2).

Returns ``{"entries": [...], "count": N, "limit": limit,
"include_replayed": bool}``. Each entry is serialised with PII
stripping (preview fields removed from the payload). The store
is the single source of truth — there is no in-memory cache.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max DLQ entries to return. Default 100, hard cap 1000. Only entries that have NOT been replayed are returned (pass ?include_replayed=true for the full audit history).
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max DLQ entries to return. Default 100, hard cap 1000. Only entries that have NOT been replayed are returned (pass ?include_replayed=true for the full audit history).

parameters.name: include_replayed

schema.description: When True, also return entries that have been replayed (audit history). Default False (only pending).
schema.title: Include Replayed

parameters.description: When True, also return entries that have been replayed (audit history). Default False (only pending).

schema.title: Response Admin Webhooks Dlq List Api V1 Observability Webhooks Dlq Get

post.description:
Replay a single DLQ entry (Phase 4.13B Drift 2).

Re-sends the original payload to the URL using the CURRENT
signing secret (honouring ``secret_version`` on the outbound
config row). On success, the entry is marked ``replayed_at``
and will not appear in the default list (``?include_replayed=
false``). On failure, the entry is left untouched (the operator
can retry).

Requires ``Scope.WEBHOOK_ADMIN`` (mutation, not just read).

Returns:
    ``{"dlq_id": id, "replayed": bool, "status_code": int}``.
    ``replayed`` is True when the resend returned 2xx AND the
    store marked the row replayed.

schema.title: Response Admin Webhooks Dlq Replay Api V1 Observability Webhooks Dlq  Dlq Id  Replay Post

get.description:
List audit entries with date-range filter, pagination, and format.

Returns:
  * ``format=json`` → ``application/json``: list of event dicts
  * ``format=csv`` → ``text/csv; charset=utf-8``: CSV document
  * Empty result → 200 with ``[]`` (JSON) or headers-only CSV

post.description:
Re-enable an auto-disabled outbound webhook URL.

Resets ``disabled_at`` to NULL and ``consecutive_failures`` to 0.
The next delivery attempt will go through normally; if the
endpoint is still flaky, the counter climbs again and the URL
is re-disabled at the threshold.

Query param:
    ``url``: The outbound URL to re-enable. Pass raw (the query
    string handles slashes natively) or percent-encoded.

Returns:
    ``{"url": <url>, "enabled": <bool>}`` where
    ``enabled`` is True if the row was previously disabled and
    is now active, False if it was already active (idempotent
    no-op). A 404 is returned when the URL has never been
    recorded by the dispatcher (no config row exists).

parameters.name: url

schema.description: The outbound webhook URL to re-enable. Outbound URLs contain slashes and colons (``https://host/path``) which can't appear raw in a path segment, so the URL is passed as a query parameter (``?url=<value>``). Percent-encoding is optional — Starlette decodes query params once.
schema.title: Url

parameters.description: The outbound webhook URL to re-enable. Outbound URLs contain slashes and colons (``https://host/path``) which can't appear raw in a path segment, so the URL is passed as a query parameter (``?url=<value>``). Percent-encoding is optional — Starlette decodes query params once.

schema.title: Response Enable Webhook Api V1 Webhooks Enable Post

get.description:
Fetch one job by id. 404 if not found.

Phase 1.6: requires ``agents.read`` scope. The token is not
used by the handler body but its presence is enforced by
the dependency — FastAPI will resolve it before the handler
runs.

List the ``recent`` most recent jobs (default 20, newest first).

Phase 1.6: requires ``agents.read`` scope.

schema.title: Response List Jobs Api V1 Agents Jobs Get

post.description:
Enqueue a sub-agent job (Phase 1.6).

Phase 1.6 scope contract:
  * Always requires ``agents.write``.
  * Additionally requires ``agents.pr`` when ``pr_mode != "off"``
    (compound check, enforced explicitly in the handler body).

Validation:
  * ``prompt`` is non-empty (Pydantic)
  * ``agent`` must be a known spec (built-in or project override)
  * ``model`` (if given) must be in the catalog
  * ``pr_mode`` is one of off/draft/ready

Response: 201 with ``job_id``, ``status='queued'``. The actual
job runs in a background ``asyncio.Task`` via the lifespan-
instantiated ``MergeQueue``.

get.description:
Phase 2.4: fetch a stack by its ``pr_stack_id``.

Returns the parent orchestrator row (``stack_position=0``) and
the ordered list of children (one per slice). 404 if no row
matches ``stack_id``. The parent's status reflects the
aggregate (``pr_open`` while children are in flight, ``merged``
after ``all_stack_children_merged`` is True, ``failed`` if
the stack was cascade-cancelled).

Phase 1.6: requires ``agents.read`` scope.

Ops health endpoint: per-repo lock stats + recent job count.

The lock stats are read from ``app.state.merge_queue`` when
available; the recent-job count is read from the store. The
two are independent so a missing queue doesn't kill the
health response.

Phase 1.6: requires ``agents.read`` scope (it's a read
endpoint, and we keep the auth surface uniform across the
v1 namespace — operators who want a fully-open health
endpoint can hit ``/api/health`` which remains legacy-open).

Return the server's self-description.

Public on purpose — this is the discovery endpoint. The
``auth_required`` flag tells the client whether subsequent
calls will need a token; the ``scopes_available`` array
is the closed set the server recognises; ``endpoints``
is built live from the app's mounted routes via
:func:`harness.server.auth.route_registry.collect_endpoints`.

Search the 4-layer memory with BM25 + identity rerank.

Query parameters:
  * ``q`` — required, the search string
  * ``k`` — optional, default 5, max 50

Returns 422 on missing ``q`` (handled by FastAPI's required-
query-param validation) and 200 with an empty ``hits`` list
on no matches.

post.description:
Dual-write a new memory note to the unified facade.

Pydantic enforces non-empty ``text`` and a sane ``layer``.
The bridge does the actual write; this route just shapes
the response.

get.description:
Return per-layer entry counts (cheap, best-effort).

The ``L1`` count is read from the on-disk JSONL file
(one line per entry). Other layers report ``available: true``
— a precise per-entry count would require a per-adapter
``count()`` method (Phase 3 follow-up).

List the most recently updated sessions (Phase 1.6).

Phase 1.6: requires ``sessions.read`` scope. The shape is
the same as the legacy ``/api/sessions`` route so a client
can swap endpoints by changing only the prefix.

schema.title: Response List Sessions V1 Api V1 Sessions Get

post.description:
Force-compact a session's context (Phase 3 v1.4.0).

Requires the ``sessions.write`` scope (separate from
``agents.write`` and ``memory.write`` because /compact is a
session-control operation).

Returns a JSON body with the same shape as
:class:`harness.context.compaction.CompactResult`:
``{"original_tokens": int, "compacted_tokens": int,
   "summary_preview": str, "cache_hit": bool,
   "saved_tokens": int}``.

Returns 503 if the compactor / trigger is not wired into the
app (e.g. dev mode without a real compactor). Returns 200 with
the result on success.

schema.title: Response Compact Session V1 Api V1 Sessions  Session Id  Compact Post

post.description:
Receive a single inbound GitHub webhook event.

Pipeline (each step short-circuits with the appropriate HTTP code):
  1. Read the raw body (bytes — we need the unparsed body
     for HMAC verification).
  2. Reject if body > ``webhook_max_payload_kb``.
  3. Read ``X-Hub-Signature-256`` / ``X-GitHub-Event`` /
     ``X-GitHub-Delivery`` headers (case-insensitive).
  4. Reject if ``webhook_secret`` is empty (503).
  5. Delegate to ``WebhookHandler.handle_raw`` (HMAC verify
     + idempotency + parse + record). On HMAC fail → 401.
  6. If the event was a redelivery (None from handle_raw),
     return 200 with ``processed: false`` and ``detail`` set.
  7. Otherwise dispatch to the JobStore via
     ``WebhookHandler.dispatch_event``.
  8. Return 200 with the result.

The route deliberately returns 200 on most "no work to do"
cases (unknown event types, no matching job, etc.) so
GitHub doesn't retry — only true error conditions (bad
signature, malformed JSON, missing secret) return non-200.

get.description:
List all registered hooks (builtin + custom) with on/off state.

Returns hooks sorted by event, then by priority.

Get a single hook by id.

Returns 404 if the hook does not exist.

post.description:
Enable a hook.

Returns 404 if the hook does not exist. Idempotent — calling
enable on an already-enabled hook succeeds (enabled=True).

Disable a hook.

Returns 404 if the hook does not exist. Idempotent — calling
disable on an already-disabled hook succeeds (enabled=False).

get.description: List all loaded plugins with their enabled/disabled state.

Get a single plugin by name.

Returns 404 if the plugin is not loaded and not in the
disabled set.

post.description:
Re-enable a previously disabled plugin.

Removes the administrative block. The plugin must be reloaded
via the loader for its hooks/tools to become active again —
this endpoint only clears the disabled flag.

Returns 200 if the plugin was disabled and is now re-enabled.
Returns 200 with ``enabled=True`` if it was already active
(idempotent). Returns 404 if the plugin name is completely
unknown.

Disable a plugin: unload + mark disabled.

Removes the plugin's hooks and tools from the registry and
marks it as disabled so it cannot be re-loaded until
``enable`` is called.

Returns 200 if the plugin was loaded and is now disabled.
Returns 404 if the plugin is not currently loaded.

get.description:
List available plugins in the marketplace.

Supports optional ``keyword`` filtering (case-insensitive
substring match against name, description, and keywords)
and ``limit`` / ``offset`` pagination.

Get a single plugin manifest by name.

Returns 404 if the plugin is not found in the marketplace.

HookDetail.title: HookDetail
HookDetail.description:
Public-facing hook info exposed via the API.

Deliberately excludes the ``callable`` reference (not JSON-
serialisable) and internal fields like ``script_path`` / ``url``
/ ``headers`` / ``model`` / ``prompt`` (those are visible in
``transport_specific``).

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Optional session scope (informational — broker is process-global).

answer.title: Answer
answer.description: The user's answer. Empty string is allowed (treated as a real answer).

LongPollAnswer.title: LongPollAnswer
LongPollAnswer.description:
Body schema for ``POST /api/v1/elicitation/answer``.

``session_id`` is optional — the broker keys questions by
``question_id`` (process-global UUID hex), so session scoping is
informational only. It's accepted for API symmetry with the
long-poll query parameter.

MarketplacePluginDetail.title: MarketplacePluginDetail
MarketplacePluginDetail.description: Public-facing plugin manifest exposed via the marketplace API.

PluginDetail.title: PluginDetail
PluginDetail.description:
Public-facing plugin info exposed via the API.

Mirrors :class:`~harness.plugins.PluginInfo` with the addition
of ``enabled`` (derived from the registry's disabled set) and
without internal fields.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: A chat session — one conversation thread with one model.

_CapabilitiesResponse.title: _CapabilitiesResponse
_CapabilitiesResponse.description: JSON shape of the capabilities response.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: Sub-agent name (built-in or project override). Default: 'explore' (read-only, safe default).

model.title: Model
model.description: Override the agent's default model. Must be a catalog id. Default: agent's spec.model.

pr_mode.title: Pr Mode
pr_mode.description: Phase 2.2: PR mode. One of 'off' (local ff-merge), 'draft' (open a draft PR), 'ready' (open a ready PR). Requires agents.pr scope when not 'off'.

pr_target.title: Pr Target
pr_target.description: Phase 2.2: target branch for the PR. Default: settings.pr_default_target_branch.

background.title: Background
background.description: Enqueue and return a job_id (default true). If false, the route runs the agent synchronously and returns the result inline.

split_into.title: Split Into
split_into.description: Phase 2.4: split the job's diff into N stacked PRs. Each slice's PR targets the previous slice's branch. Requires ``pr_mode`` != 'off' (stacks need gh). Default: null (no split, single-PR path).

split_strategy.title: Split Strategy
split_strategy.description: Phase 2.4: split strategy override. One of 'auto', 'files', 'directory', 'size'. Default: settings.pr_split_strategy = 'auto'.

stack_id.title: Stack Id
stack_id.description: Phase 2.4: stack identifier. If omitted, the queue generates a new one. Use to re-enqueue an existing stack with the same id (rare; usually managed by the orchestrator).

stack_repos.title: Stack Repos
stack_repos.description: Phase 2.5: one absolute repo path per slice, for cross-repo stacks. ``len(stack_repos)`` MUST equal ``split_into`` when both are set. Default: null (single-repo stack — all slices live in the worktree repo, Phase 2.4 behaviour).

_QueueHealth.title: _QueueHealth
_QueueHealth.description: JSON shape of the merge-queue health endpoint.

_StackSchema.title: _StackSchema
_StackSchema.description: JSON shape of ``GET /stacks/{stack_id}`` — a parent + children.

_WebhookAck.title: _WebhookAck
_WebhookAck.description:
Response shape for a successfully accepted webhook.

Always returns 200 OK. ``processed`` is True when the
handler did meaningful work (e.g. marked a job merged);
False for redeliveries, unknown event types, and
"we saw this but there's nothing to do" cases.
